Scenario.overview: Healthcare and Public Health organizations Simple This scenario depicts a straightforward ransomware attack targeting sensitive data in the healthcare sector, utilizing phishing for initial access followed by indirect command execution and reversible encryption for impact.

threat_actor_profile.motivation: Financial gain through ransom demands, coupled with disruption of services to extort affected entities.

Scenario.attack_surface: AVAILABLE_SURFACES: Email systems, Web applications, Active Directory components
Scenario.attack_vector: AVAILABLE_VECTORS: Social engineering via email, Exploitation of command execution flaws, Manipulation of directory services

stage.name: Initial Access
stage.description: The attacker initiates contact through internet-facing assets by deploying a mobile phishing campaign, sending deceptive SMS messages or emails that mimic legitimate healthcare providers to trick recipients into revealing login credentials, thus exploiting human factors rather than complex system vulnerabilities.

stage.name: Execution
stage.capec: CAPEC-9: Buffer Overflow in Local Command-Line Utilities
stage.description: Upon gaining access to credentials, the attacker leverages a buffer overflow vulnerability in locally hosted command-line utilities to execute malicious commands indirectly, allowing the deployment of ransomware payloads without direct user interaction, capitalizing on the ability to manipulate system processes remotely.

stage.name: Impact
stage.description: The attacker modifies objects within Active Directory using reversible encryption techniques, encrypting critical electronic health record files and public health datasets to disrupt normal operations, demand payment for decryption keys, and maximize damage through service interruptions and data unavailability.

Scenario.summary: A targeted ransomware attack beginning with phishing exploits, escalating through command manipulation, and ending with encrypted data exfiltration and disruption in the healthcare sector.
